WHEN HE OVERTHREW THE CITIES IN WHICH LOT DWELT. In one of which Lot had been living.49Lot did not dwell in all of the overthrown cities. He dwelt only in Sodom. Each of these is similar: Then died Jephthah the Gileadite, and was buried in the cities of Gilead (Jud. 12:7);50Its meaning is: and was buried in one of the cities of Gilead. And Adam begat thirty sons and thirty v. 1 daughters and Adam lived nine hundred and thirty years; and he fell sick and cried with a loud 2 voice and said, ‘ Let all my sons come to me that I may see them before I die.’

sons, and thirty daughters, whom he sent abroad, and took in thirty daughters from abroad for his sons?” It will probably be quite impossible to find one solitary case of a man who has kept the first com- mandment in righteousness, like these judges of Israel, and other good men of former days. i
In place of such men, thousands may be found who believe that a life of celi- bacy is one of peculiar…
